name: Cloudflare XXE Detection Baseline Pack
objective: Detect and disrupt XML External Entity (XXE) attack attempts across XML request bodies and query parameters using high-signal baseline signatures.
risk_mitigated: XXE exploitation leading to sensitive file disclosure, SSRF pivoting through XML parsers, and parser-level denial of service from malicious external entity declarations.

production_defaults.notes: Start in log mode for at least 7 days, then move to managed challenge for non-browser/API traffic. Promote to block after validating parser and client behavior in production telemetry.

rules.description: Detect XML DTD/entity declarations in request body.
Detect SYSTEM/PUBLIC external identifier usage in XML body.
Detect file:// and expect:// entity reference indicators in XML body.
Detect XXE markers in query string parameters.
rules.expression: (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "<!doctype") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "<!entity") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "system") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "public") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "file://") or (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "expect://")

tuning_notes: Scope rules to XML endpoints first (for example, SOAP, XML-RPC, SAML ACS) to reduce noise.
Allowlist known-safe integrations that legitimately send DTD-like content.
If false positives occur on generic 'system' or 'public' tokens, keep those detections in log mode while enforcing stronger indicators (DOCTYPE/ENTITY and scheme-based references).
Pair with parser hardening (disable external entities, disable DTD processing where possible) for defense in depth.
deployment_notes: Deploy in log mode first and validate events by path, ASN, JA3/JA4, user-agent, and source reputation.
Promote high-confidence signatures (DOCTYPE/ENTITY + file:// or expect://) to managed challenge before blocking.
Use phased rollout by route/hostname and maintain rollback capability.
Coordinate with application owners for XML parser behavior confirmation before block enforcement.
monitoring_guidance: Track hit-rate per rule ID and endpoint to identify concentrated probing.
Alert on spikes in cf-xxe-001/cf-xxe-003 combinations from single sources or distributed low-and-slow campaigns.
Monitor challenge solve/fail ratios after moving from log to managed_challenge.
Review sampled payloads weekly to refine endpoint scoping and allowlists.

terraform_example.description: Concise example aligned to Cloudflare custom ruleset conventions; start with log/challenge actions.
